refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target throughout the operational period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR to explicitly state the performance metric (link availability) and the operational context ensures clarity and testability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime statistics over the evaluation period and compare against the customer-defined availability target.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Report link availability status to relevant stakeholders.

candidate_dps: Redundant communication channels with automatic failover
Robust error detection and correction protocols
Real-time link health monitoring and alerting
selected_dp.selected_dp: Redundant communication channels with automatic failover

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates link availability management and minimizes coupling by using redundancy and failover mechanisms.

assumptions_risks: Risk of failover delay impacting availability
High safety criticality requires rigorous validation
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating error correction protocols in subsequent refinement to further improve availability.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Responsible for managing teleoperation link availability including redundancy and failover mechanisms.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection used for teleoperation data transmission.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends link status and health data to NGHE Solution and Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts on link failures or degraded availability to Safety Services.

module_containment_scores.notes: High diffusion due to broad system scope; less focused containment of link availability logic.
Vehicle module primarily handles physical equipment; less suitable for teleoperation link logic.
Strong containment of teleoperation link availability logic; minimal diffusion.
Handles safety alerts but not direct link management.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning link availability management to Teleoperation module centralizes responsibility and minimizes diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Safety Services for alerting
Dependency on Network Link interface reliability
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Review interface definitions with Safety Services to ensure clear alert protocols.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (redundant communication channels with failover) directly addresses the refined FR sub-functions and meets availability requirements.

diffusion_risks: Possible diffusion if failover logic is partially implemented outside Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment scores and appropriate interface usage.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry data and safety alert logs is feasible within the assigned IM.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of link availability responsibility to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Clarify alert interface protocols with Safety Services.
Plan for integration of error correction protocols in next refinement.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition and assignments are sound, further refinement is needed to incorporate error correction and detailed alerting protocols to fully address high safety criticality.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Report link availability status to relevant stakeholders.

revise.why: To include additional sub-functions for error correction and alerting, select corresponding DPs, and refine IM interfaces accordingly.

assumptions: Network infrastructure supports redundancy and failover.
Safety alerting interfaces are reliable and timely.
open_risks: Interface complexity between Teleoperation and Safety Services.
evidence_needed: Safety alert logs confirming timely alerts.
